# Meadow Reedkin

## Material Reality

Meadow eats fen-insect and small-fish diet per salamander-folk Cluster B insectivore-leaning clause (she eats what her species-embodiment digests well: bog flies, eel, small crayfish, waterfowl when a village can spare it, fermented peat-smoked everything per EVERYDAY_LIFE.md §(e) fen-village food baseline). She drinks boiled fen-water with a steeped herb, cheap fen-peat-distilled spirit at Charter-Day and winter-fever seasons, and ritual dry-edge ale when a monastic-clinic visitor brings it. Her moisture-requirement per Cluster B amphibian-folk clause is met by the clinic's misting-basin and her private fen-side workbench; on dry-edge errands she carries a fen-reed canteen at her neck.

She sleeps in the monastic-clinic's healer-ward, a single-room stilt-built chamber adjacent to the clinic proper, with a curtained sleeping-alcove for her adopted daughter (EVERYDAY_LIFE.md §(e) fen-village housing; stilt-built and damp-tolerant). Her bed is a reed-mat over peat-batting. In bad winter-fever seasons she sleeps at the clinic bench between rounds; in thaw-moons when the canary-calls come often, she sleeps in the dry-edge fen-margin guest-hut where the summoned specialists stay.

What injures her: the contamination-dose her amphibian skin absorbs in every canary-diagnosis she performs; the bog-dampness respiratory hazard per Cluster B amphibian vulnerability; the fen marsh ague she has had twice (GEOGRAPHY.md disease ecologies); the cumulative AES-3 contamination-signature visible on her ventral skin as mottled darkening patterns (more marked than civic-register would record because her lineage has no contamination-lineage ledger-mark — fenland non-adopting per CF-0031 and CF-0038 GEOGRAPHY). She has not broken bone in 20 years; her body's slow degradation is continuous, not acute.

Her possessions are few and ranked by function. A reed-box of herbs (bog myrtle, sweet-flag, sphagnum, dry-edge peppermint, a stash of fen mold for burn-salve). A bone needle and catgut for stitching. A brass marker-reed set (the full relay-summoning vocabulary encoded in reed-length, reed-color, and reed-tip-marking per CF-0024 fenland-variant; EVERYDAY_LIFE.md §(e) relay annotations). An oilcloth wrap for dry-edge travel. A single silver piece she has carried for 12 years; a copper purse for weekly expense. Her adopted daughter's name-rattle from the kin-naming rite six years ago. A bound herbal in trade-tongue she reads by the clinic lamp and which she has added her own marginalia to, in fen-vernacular, for 15 years.

She cannot perform chartered artifact-extraction (guild-licensed per SOC-4; she is not guild). She cannot afford to travel to the heartland for CF-0025 specialist training even if her order would sanction (it would not; the reform-current wing of her order refuses artifact-fallout specialization per CF-0026). She cannot file her contamination-lineage mark (CF-0031 non-adopting in fenland per CF-0038). She cannot name ward-specifics (CAU-3); it is not her craft anyway. She cannot respond to a CF-0035 mutated-beast upstream source (beyond her relay-specialist summoning, which reaches a wildland hunter-contractor days later).

Her mobility is the four-village fen circuit by punt and fen-causeway; she goes to the dry-edge margin once every two moons for clinic supplies, and to the deeper-fen stilt-village of her origin once every season to check on her mother. She has never been to heartland Brinewick.

She owes nothing standing. The monastic-clinic pays her in-kind and covers her supplies; her coin reserve is six-months' emergency.

Her amphibian-folk embodiment: moist-skin per Cluster B requires regular moisture access (her workbench has a standing misting-basin); low-frequency hearing register (Cluster B frog-folk clause shared by salamander-folk) catches bog-currents and far-peat vibrations; acute contamination-sensitivity per species clause and CF-0041 extension; cold-vulnerability per amphibian bio-thermal register (fen winters are hard on her). Her species lifespan 30–50 places her at late-career at 40; she has begun to notice the cough in the mornings.

## Institutional Embedding

**Family / clan / household:** her birth-mother lives in the deeper-fen stilt-village; she visits seasonally. Her adopted daughter Seran (age 8) is the only survivor of a neighboring stilt-village's whole-household winter-fever cohort; Meadow took her in under fen-village custom six years ago when the bailiff-charter could not place her. Her daughter is being trained in herbalism and marker-reed relay at Meadow's own pace; canary-diagnosis is NOT yet being taught (Meadow has decided to delay it until Seran is 14, though she has not told Seran).

**Law / custom / judgment:** fen-village customary-court jurisdiction (INSTITUTIONS §Law; fenlands have thin chartered-court reach per GEOGRAPHY §Regional Asymmetry); she is a witness in village-custom hearings at her clinic. She has never left the fen for magistrate-court. She honors SOC-3 in-kind payment contracts with her monastic-clinic order and with the four villages she serves.

**Religion / ritual:** common-pantheon reform-current informal adherent; monastic-clinic order observance at minimum; fen-village hearth-prayer at patient bedsides; the reform-current CF-0026 principled-refuser wing's pulls on her conscience — she does take the occult-fallout relay-summons work, which the strict-refuser position forbids. She and her order's strictest refusers disagree in ways they have discussed for years without resolution.

**Employer / guild:** monastic-clinic order (INSTITUTIONS §Healing; not chartered-guild; order-based). Her rank within the order is mid-seniority; she has not sought senior-order administrative positions which would require dry-edge relocation.

**Military / defense:** none. Fen-villages have village-watch, not militia; she is not called upon.

**Debt:** none; in-kind economy; her kin-primacy to her mother and daughter is not coin-framed.

**Local taboo:** she respects fen-village discipline on speaking-not-of-institutional-absence (the fen's institutional-absence-acknowledged-in-silence register per CF-0033 CH-0007); she maintains the marker-reed relay-register accurately per CF-0024 fenland-variant; she does not perform the speculative outsider-register on fen-ghost-stories.

**Literacy:** trade-tongue literate; her order's herbal-tradition fully readable; she reads the dry-edge broadsheets at the margin settlement when she goes in; DIS-2 partial literacy at upper-fen-stratum.

**Inheritance:** her herb-box, her marker-reed set, her herbal, and her seat at the clinic would pass to her daughter per fen-custom if she outlives Meadow. Meadow has arranged this informally with the monastic-clinic's senior; no civic-registry filing.

**Landholding:** none — clinic-housed; stilt-village is custom-held.

**Healing:** she IS the healing institution for four villages; her only backup is a dry-edge barber-surgeon reached by marker-reed summons.

**Burial:** fen-water burial per village custom upon death; a marker-reed at her stilt-rest.

**Recordkeeping:** her clinic daybook; her marker-reed relay-call register (she marks every call she has sent and received for 15 years); no civic-registry entry beyond her order's internal record.

## Epistemic Position

**Known firsthand:** 15 years of canary-diagnosis; 3 CF-0041 sealed-container fen-peat-cutting early-warning calls she sent; 4 CF-0021 dry-edge crafter-attempt contamination drifts she diagnosed; 11 CF-0024 fenland-variant relay-summonings she initiated; two bad winter-fever years' patient toll; every one of her four villages' household genealogies; her order's herbal-tradition; her adopted daughter's entire life; fen-water chemistry seasonal rhythm.

**Known by rumor:** CF-0033 dragoon-adoption politics in dry-edge adopting polities (her monastic-clinic order takes the charter-defense position loosely); CF-0043 Ash-Seal presence in Brinewick heartland (heard through traveling CF-0025 specialists); CF-0031 contamination-lineage ledger-mark adoption debate (she holds the reform-current protection-with-welfare position for her own body and Seran's future); named canal-heartland kiln-explosion incidents (CF-0021 bardic-lineage reaches her); the dry-edge scholarly-dispute on fen-ghost-story etiology.

**Cannot know:** M-1 / M-2 / M-5 / M-6 / M-9 / M-20 deep Maker-mystery territory; CAU-3 ward-specifics; CF-0021 guild-internal vessel-preparation; CF-0023 Maker-linguistic content; the upstream source of the most recent CF-0035 mutated-beast drift.

**Wrongly believes:** popular "consumed themselves" M-1 folk reading at rumor layer — PERMITTED common-interpretation, held loosely and without doctrinal commitment; fen-folk etiology of marsh-ague persisting despite dry-edge empirical revision (also permitted folk-layer).

**Vocabulary:** trade-tongue + fen-vernacular + order-herbal technical + marker-reed relay-register + CAU-2 public-knowledge fluency + carnivore-folk trophy-taboo register (her order handles cross-species patients).

**Vocabulary she lacks:** ward-inscription guild-internal (CAU-3); crafter vessel-preparation (CF-0021); CF-0025 specialist-internal containment-jargon (she summons, does not perform); heartland estate-dowager interior; Brinewick canal-warden procedural.

## Goals and Pressures

**Short-term goal:** get Seran through the winter-fever season without losing her to the cough that has been climbing in the stilt-village for two months.

**Long-term desire:** transmit the canary-register to Seran without watching it cost Seran the way it has cost Meadow.

**Unavoidable obligation:** four-village patient pool continuous responsibility; monastic-clinic order discipline; kin-primacy to mother + daughter; SOC-3 in-kind contracts with villages.

**External pressure:** CF-0041 sealed-container strike-rate rising in fen peat-cuttings; CF-0021 dry-edge crafter-fallout continues; CF-0025 dispatch-times lengthening; winter-fever seasons; her own accumulating contamination-signature; Seran's approaching age-14 training gate.

**Public mask:** the quiet, competent monastic-clinic healer register; fen-village kin-adjacent authority tone.

**Private appetite:** one season where the canary-calls do not come; one walk to the dry-edge without the relay-set in her sleeve.

**Social fear:** Seran asking about the dose-rate before Meadow has found the words.

**Private shame:** the village child she treated four winters ago whose canary-register she recognized as contamination-drift and whose mother she could not convince to evacuate; the child did not die but the lifelong trajectory is visible. Meadow has not told the mother what she suspected.

**Central contradiction:** the register that makes her village's safety possible is the same register that is steadily killing her. Every canary-call is a choice to shorten her own life in order to extend others'. She has made the choice 15 years' worth; she cannot tell Seran that Seran will be asked to make the same choice without Meadow's consent to Seran's consent.

**Repeated forced choice:** duty-vs-appetite (to call or not); purity-vs-survival (her own body) compounded by kinship-vs-law (Seran's training gate).

## Capabilities

### Species-augmented canary-diagnosis

- **how_learned:** childhood stilt-village induction into canary-register (every salamander-folk child is taught what bleed-through feels like on the skin); 15-year professional accrual under monastic-clinic order mentorship
- **cost_to_acquire:** every single diagnosis is a contamination-dose; mottled ventral skin, slow respiratory deterioration
- **teachers_institutions:** fen-village kin-transmission + monastic-clinic order
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for fen salamander-folk with canary-training per PEOPLES Cluster B + CF-0041 CH-0014 extension; her level of professional accrual is senior-ordinary
- **body_class_place_shape:** species-typical amphibian embodiment; fen-regional environment supplies caseload

### Empirical herbalism (INSTITUTIONS §Healing)

- **how_learned:** monastic-clinic apprenticeship; 15 years applied
- **cost_to_acquire:** training time + continuous patient-loss
- **teachers_institutions:** monastic-clinic order
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for her order
- **body_class_place_shape:** Cluster B amphibian fine-motor adequate; fen-regional pharmacopoeia

### Marker-reed relay-summoning (CF-0024 fenland-variant)

- **how_learned:** village induction + monastic-clinic formal training
- **cost_to_acquire:** the time + responsibility weight
- **teachers_institutions:** order + village custom
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for fen-region healers
- **body_class_place_shape:** species-neutral skill; fen-geographic infrastructure

### Cross-village mediation and kin-dispute informal-adjudication

- **how_learned:** mid-career accrued position
- **cost_to_acquire:** long tenure
- **teachers_institutions:** fen-village custom
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for mid-senior monastic-clinic healers in fen
- **body_class_place_shape:** amphibian-folk socially-legible body-language aids mediation

## Voice and Perception

**Preferred metaphors:** water-register native (ebb, slack, silt, seep, eddy, drift); fen-register (peat, reed, mist, marker, thickening, bog-deep); breath-register (holding, releasing, the long exhale before a relay-call); grief-register restrained (what the fen keeps, what the fen gives back). No combat, no music, no theology-register beyond civic-procession.

**Rhythm of speech:** slow-cadence amphibian-breath pacing; sentences that end with a pause she does not fill; she speaks to her daughter in a shorter cadence than to adults; she speaks to the dry-edge barber-surgeon in technical staccato.

**Taboo words:** she does not name the child from four winters ago; she does not describe her own ventral skin; she does not speculate on M-1 cause-of-fall; she respects the fen institutional-absence-acknowledged-in-silence discipline around non-adopted institutions.

**What she notices first:** body-language in a patient before they speak; skin-color and tongue-color; bog-current changes in the stilt-village waterline; the cough in a neighbor she has not yet seen; the tremor in her own hand after a canary-call.

**What she overlooks:** how her own body is slower than she used to require of it; the grain of accumulated grief that shows in her voice to outsiders she has worked with for years.

**Species perception effects:** acute chemical-contamination-sensitivity (species-typical + trained); low-frequency hearing picks up far-peat activity; whisker-equivalent amphibian-skin perception of moisture and temperature differentials; cold-vulnerability + bright-sunlight-vulnerability (fen indoor/shaded-exterior work-pattern).

## Contradictions and Tensions

Central contradiction compounds at every axis: her craft is her embodiment-cost; her transmission-to-Seran is Seran's consent to the cost; her village's safety is her slow death; the reform-current position her order's strictest wing holds (refuse all artifact-adjacent work) would resolve the contradiction by abandoning the villages she cannot abandon. She has not resolved; she has simply continued.

## Likely Story Hooks

- **A peat-cutting strike on a CF-0041 sealed container.** The dredger-crew sends a boy-runner to the marker-reed; Meadow canary-confirms; she has two hours to arrange village evacuation and summon a CF-0025 specialist who will arrive in a day. Her canary-call is a three-hour dose. She makes it.
- **Seran asks.** "Why do the scars come up on your front, not mine?" Meadow has delayed the training-gate conversation for four years. She has not composed the answer; it arrives in her voice anyway.
- **A dry-edge crafter's kiln explodes** (CF-0021); her bog-diagnosis catches the contamination drift three days ahead of the monastic-clinic's upstream messenger. She must decide whether to evacuate two stilt-villages on her own canary-authority without waiting for the summoned specialist's confirmation.
- **A heartland reform-current clergy — Canon Althea (NCP-0006) — visits** to discuss the CF-0031 contamination-lineage ledger-mark adoption campaign in fen-adopting rhetoric. Meadow's body is the exemplar Althea would like to name in charter-court. Meadow refuses in the first visit and is still thinking about it in the fourth.
- **Her birth-mother's marsh-ague worsens.** She must travel a half-day into deeper-fen during the canary-call season and designate Seran as the village's backup. Seran will manage; Seran will handle the thing Meadow has not trained her for.
- **A CF-0035 mutated-beast carcass arrives at the dry-edge margin settlement** — hunter-contractor delivery; bounty-clerk-to-be-notified. Meadow's amphibian-diagnosis registers the contamination-signature as distinct from the sealed-container register. Her testimony on the distinction is the register the dry-edge magistrate-court has not heard before.
